pattern: Folding the array with XOR yields the XOR of the two unique values; an isolated set bit of that combined value splits every number into two groups, each containing exactly one of the loners.
hints: If you XOR everything together the paired values cancel and you are left with the XOR of the two answers combined.
Any bit that is set in that combined XOR is a bit where the two answers differ, so it can separate them.
Pick one such set bit, partition all numbers by whether they have that bit, and XOR each partition independently to recover the two unique values.

edge_inputs.description: minimum length is just the two unique values
two positives surrounded by a cancelling pair
the two singles are adjacent in value
one single is negative, one positive
both singles negative among cancelling pairs
extreme 32-bit magnitudes as the two loners

stress.description: many random cancelling pairs plus two planted distinct singles, shuffled
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, size)
    pairs = (n - 2) // 2
    used = set()
    arr = []
    while len(used) < pairs:
        v = rng.randrange(-2**31, 2**31)
        if v in used:
            continue
        used.add(v)
        arr.append(v)
        arr.append(v)
    singles = []
    while len(singles) < 2:
        u = rng.randrange(-2**31, 2**31)
        if u in used:
            continue
        used.add(u)
        singles.append(u)
    arr.extend(singles)
    rng.shuffle(arr)
    return [arr]
stress.note: exactly two values with odd multiplicity by construction
